title: Brachypodium tenellum
taxonomy: Brachypodium tenellum is classified as a member of the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This classification is based on its characteristics and morphological features, such as its seed structure and leaf arrangement. As a member of the genus Brachypodium, B. tenellum is closely related to other species within this genus, which includes B. distachyum and B. sylvaticum. The family Poaceae is further classified within the monocot clade, which includes other flowering plants such as orchids and lilies. B. tenellum's classification reflects its evolutionary history and its position within the plant kingdom.

intro: Brachypodium tenellum, also known as slender false brome, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and North Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Brachypodium tenellum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Brachypodium tenellum is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is used to treat respiratory issues, as well as to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
